review_text: Stayed two nights in a two bedroom cabin.  The rooms were clean with fresh linen and sufficient bedding provided. Kitchen had all the basics for quick meal preparation eg 2 burner cooktop, microwave, toaster, jug, saucepans, frying pan. Reverse cycle AC and wood available for the fireplace made for a cosy May stay. Good reading lights in the bedroom and plenty of hot water for showers. Staff were friendly and helpful.
Excellent place for a weekend getaway or holiday.
Very good place to stay

Great position to use as a home base, not far to town center.
Would suggest you ask what sort of site you're getting, quite a few with no slabs, alot slope abit much, and the wrong way which makes your first step into ya caravan a stretch. Amenities could of been better cleaned 🙄
Two night stay in the cabin, great spot and great service. You can buy a box of wood for $10 at the front desk that is perfect amount for one night. Will stay again.

Great park and close to everything needed, i.e. shops, servo, etc. Facilities are great, 3 camp kitchens with the main one enclosed so no bothersome flies. Ladies was kept immaculate and the showers had great pressure and hot water. 2nd shower cubicle in I found to be the best 😊 If I'm ever back that way would definitely stay again.
Very clean facilities. Great spot. Some Caravan  spots are quite unlevel and the bitumen roads within the park are in appalling disrepair.

Tourist Park well placed with sanitary facilities and clean common areas. The sites are quite good and quite shady, but it's a shame that the roads are in very bad condition to access them.

However, we were disappointed to learn that the prices (for the same duration and the same locations) were not the same for everyone. Sites with electricity indicated as $42 on your site with supposedly no locations without electricity. But some pay $27 per night, others $32 per night and unfortunately we didn't have that chance because we paid $42 for a site without water or electricity.

Too bad to give a price to the customer's head

We stayed three nights at the Top of the Town Tourist Park on our way to Crows Nest. It was the best decision we made. With powered and unpowered sites available we choose an unpowered site, as we have enough solar and battery power. The campground is much more spacious as it appears from the outside. As the campground backs onto a hill it's great to have campsites at the bottom and up on different levels. There are comon fireplaces for all to use and firewood supplied. Also a great camp kitchen with indoor and outdoor seating. The amenities  male and female are very clean and well looked after, a great deal better than other places we have seen. If you travelling through Stanthorpe or surrounding area, I highly recommend this campground.

Great little caravan park.
Very clean amenities complete with some fresh looking green plants.
Good pool for a cool down in the afternoon and close to everything in town

Lovely van park north of town. Clean and well maintained with good grassy sites and a great area at back for campers. 5 minutes to town for pubs, cafes, pharmacy eetc.Easy drive to Girraween National Park.
Great spot for a one night caravan stay and look around town

A good place to have a break. Rooms and amenities are clean and do the job nicely. The front driveway entrance and certain parts in the park need fixing. Becoming a little ptoceu now, so for it to be a better place to stay , these areas need addressing

This is a must if travelling in a van. It is like a mini Girraween on the edge of town. Nice staff, very clean, handy location to everything, quiet as a church mouse at night, very good facilities, plenty of space and a great lookout. Handy to town, Prices are very reasonable also. Dog friendly.
Really enjoy staying here.

Can't comment on rooms as we stayed in our van. Enjoyed our stay very much, staff were lovely, amenities were mostly clean as the park was very busy. Some work needed on driveway and roads but there was a water leak, probably not able to be fixed due to Xmas period and most likely going to be a big fix! Overall, I would definitely stay again so relaxed and lots of friendly guests as well 😊
Stayed in a powered site for five nights. Amenities were always clean, beautiful bush up the back of the park, and it’s in a great location close to town. Really enjoyed our stay.

We had a great time here. Excellent service over the phone and in person. We were very pleased they organised our large group with 5 different accommodation needs so well, with a fantastic ensuited site for us personally. Camp kitchen was excellent too. Highly recommend, thank you!

Good value country camp ground.
Showing its age but for the price, worth it.
Young Hayley at reception was wonderful, she got us set up in a great spot.
The park is on the side of a hill so has a few different terraced areas for tent, campers, caravans.
Easy to drive our 21 ft caravan around.
Some neat bushy areas for tents.
Drive through sites.
Plenty of cabins.
Bbq areas.
Camp kitchens.
Laundry.
Dump point.
Right next door to the showgrounds, which are not always open for caravans.
There is a little pool which looked very clean, though it was cold the day we stayed.
A short cycle into town.

We stayed at this park 21/11 to 23/11 2025 in a caravan. This caravan park has all we need and expect from a caravan park. Reception staff were friendly and helpful. There are lots of sites to choose from, some are rather small, 55 - 60 m2 in our case. Concrete slabs in many cases. There are more than one amenity  block. The one close to us was modern, all tiles and stainless steel set up and very clean. Plenty of showers, toilets and hand basins. We were impressed. There is a camp kitchen, pool/TV room and a nice little swimming pool. One can walk to town in 20 minutes, it is uphill coming home so give it 25!
Beautiful park,  clean amenities, owners/management are lovely and very helpful the kids loved the pool overall just a really good experience absolutely loved it and cant wait to return
Great park with lots of different accommodation options,  nice scenery.
Good standard park, outstanding reception 😃 good cabin

Great location & friendly service. The amenities block was clean & tidy. Someone did take my liquid bath soap within 30 minutes of me forgetting it. But it was a lovely park. A nice  walk into town. We’ll be back.
Great park,close to town. Friendly staff,great facilities
Stayed on unpowered caravan site. Lovely amenities kept spotlessly clean. Great camp kitchen. Will come again.
Clean and well maintained park sites and facilities. Nice wide roadways for site access. Friendly staff.
Busy spot, comfortable, close to town.

We booked an ensuite site for our overnight stay. Great location, friendly reception staff. The ensuite had everything you need including a perfect pressured shower. Unfortunately, it also had spiders and a ton of cobwebs over the windows but you're in a bush camp so no big deal. Lovely park, lots to explore, our kids had a great time. Very peaceful place. We enjoyed our stay and would return if we are ever back that way.

Awesome location, close to town.
Better location for wifi if you need it then the places furthet out of town.
Amazing place lovely people, great facilities, the powered sites up the top were the best of all worlds with feeling private and nice, as well as having power. 10/10!!

Enjoyed 5 days at this good location. Pleasant surroundings and good camp kitchens. No room service (change of linen, towels, removal of garbage) in the 5 days we were there. Not sure if this is a criticism as we were unaware of park policy. Showers definitely needed screens of some kind.
Terrible customer service with no credit offered when extenuating circumstances stopped us from our holiday

The service has been great.  The heater was on for us when we arrived and the cabin itself is small (normal for a standard cabin) but comfortable.  There is carpet in the living area.  We have stayed in a lot of standard caravan park cabins and
carpet is a first and very welcome in this cold weather.

We stayed in the 2 bedroom cottage it was a large sized space with a full size kitchen dining lounge. Bathroom separate shower. Fully screened and 2 aircon for heating and cooling.
Wood fire (would have been great had wood and fire starters were provided in the cottage) and a Broom or vacume to clean the floor. King size and queen sized beds- great warm shower. Stacks of windows and natural light.
Little outdoor area to sit and close to the BBQ and fire area. Great cooking utensils and good sized fridge. This park has a pool and has so many wonderful spots for caravans with or without ensuite and also other small medium cabins- motel like rooms. This is right beside the show grounds and is a really nice beautiful tourist park. WiFi isn't good and the tv in the room doesn't hook up to WiFi or hot spot easily.
A great spot to stay.

We are on a powered site, very level.
The amenities are 2nd to none, and the lady in reception extremely nice and helpful, I highly recommend this park, very nice and close to town.
Great Park lots of room for the dogs to go for a walk

Just the place we needed to be. We caught up with so many family and friends Attended 3 main celebrations for Anzac Day. Attended a special Birthday celebration, and then the Markets next door. Top of the Town Park was a great place to stay and so good to visit Stanthorpe again.
Nice park , facilities a very clean

Great little park at the 'top of the town' quite literally. Easy 15 min walk to the main hub, very clean amenities and good size van spaces. Service was lovely from the staff. Great option for a stop over 😀

Really disappointed in this park. We booked and paid for 3 nights in low season. ( park virtually empty) Unfortunately after the 1st night we had to return to the Gold Coast due to a medical emergency. My wife asked if the 2 nights could be refunded or credited for future use? We don't do Refunds or Credits! No worries, we don't come back and we spread the word. Far better parks without the attitude in the area.
Quite peaceful and good views from up the back of the park.

A very large park with lots of facilities that are all clean and well maintained.
Just a little far out of the town centre.
Great location friendly atmosphere and very clean amenities
Shower block could do with more attention on weekends.
Nice clean amenities, good parking space.
Lovely dog friendly camp ground.  Clean and tidy and lots of places to camp.  We will be back
Equally best caravan park we've been to. We've decided to stay for 2 weeks. The town is a 5 minute easy walk,amenities are tidy,clean and well maintained with good staff communication and clarity.
Does the job at a reasonable price with modern and clean amenities.  Would recommend.

Nice park, clean amenities and has a pool
Very friendly staff

Lovely stay close to town, the amenities were delightful and a great camp kitchen.
Laundry available and working.
Accommodating to last min change to the schedule.

It was so hot we stayed a night to have pool have stayed many times
Their roadways need attention
The rooms are small, old, out-dated, smelly and extremely dirty. The main bed was full of ants. The bathroom was borderline unusable as it was so filthy.
Basic but great base to visit the attractions of Stanthorpe. Service from the reception was great.

A very large park with big sites, some drive through. Very quiet. Lovely clean amenities but the shower has pulsing water and can go very hot or cold sometimes. Not close enough to town to walk in, but there is a restaurant across the road and lovely take away like Thai and pizza in town. Lots of vineyards and national parks for day trips nearby.

Driving in was a shock with road being in such bad condition. The lock on the front door was very old and needed to be jiggled just right to get in. Tiny cabin with a fireplace still full of ashes. No lounge, just hard plastic dining chairs. Sheets were yellowing and had wrinkles from being already slept on. Shower had no soap, shampoo or conditioner. Shower curtain dripped onto bathroom floor that had no drain hole but the water flowed outside around the toilet pipe that wasn’t sealed to the floor. Hot water ran out after a very short shower (hot water tank was in wardrobe and was tiny). Winder on blinds in single beds room was missing and a bit of slat missing so couldn’t close for privacy. Dirty old hot plates and frypan. Kitchen blind had a broken slat and the window was filthy with a big old spider web in the corner that boasted a big dead fly. Boards on outside of building were floating in the wind above a broken pier. A massive bird poo (probably from a low flying duck) adorned the outside wall along with spider webs. I had to wipe down the table before we sat at it, that was shocking. Had to wipe surfaces and bed heads, totally putrid. I thought we might be able to lie on a lounge in the games room and watch telly but the lounges were covered in human remnants. We had booked for 2 night so I asked to get a refund for the second night as we wouldn’t be staying 2 nights. She told me that she didn’t think she could give a refund so I told her that she could. She finally agreed but kept $20 for some booking fee or something. I thought it was worth it to pay $20 to just not have to stay another night in that hellhole.
Rated this place 0 out of 10. We couldn’t find anything good about it. It’s funny because it’s a G’day Park and we rated  the one at Lake Boga 10 out of 10.
Really wrecked our few days away which we cut short. Shame on management.

Very clean amenities. Friendly staff .
Camp kitchen etc.
Easy to find next to Museum.
Pretty good little park and reasonable prices. Nothing much to amuse the kids but that was solved by a quick trip into town to the playground.
Great service. The food was delicious.
Great park would stay again.

The lady I met in reception was so very kind to me, I really appreciated it!! I come to Stanthorpe sometimes to see my mum who lives off grid, and I came here to buy a shower... Their bathrooms are SO CLEAN and the entire place has a very modern and well kept feel to it.

We stayed at an ensuite powered caravan site and this was our last stay over a two week adventure. I'd have to rate it as the best of the parks that we stayed at on our trip. The ensuite sites are large and located in a semi bushland setting, so we had seclusion and privacy. It was also quiet with very little traffic noise. Next time we'll stay longer than one night to explore the area.

Sites are large and well spaced around the granite rocks and facilities are great. The location is on the edge of town so very convenient to cafes, restaurants and easy access to the wineries and local attractions around Stanthorpe and Ballandean.
Great park, great facilities extended our stay as couldn’t miss a feed at the steak house opposite. Do yourself a favour & choose this park . Great friendly managers made it enjoy. 😊

What a lovely tourist park. Staff were lovely. The park was clean, very neat and tidy. Amenities were spotless. Couldn't really fault anything. Great location and pretty central. Such a variety of accommodation options from tenting to motel rooms.
Great place to stay
Caravan sites were extremely hard to navigate and uneven

Park is part of the Discovery chain|Has a large number of facilities like pool,playground and fire pit|Sites are good sized but tend to be muddy and grassy so bring a mat|Amenities are clean|Showers tend to get everything wet but otherwise are large
Great place. Go there.
Good clean park great staff enjoyed my stay each time

We stayed in a family cabin which had a fire to keep us warm. The cabin was comfortable and clean and had all we needed. Was a good price for what you got. The park is beautiful with some nice walks and fire pits scattered around . The relaxing quiet nature of the park is a plus. We walked across to the Aussie Beef Steakhouse across the road which was also very good. Would recommend and would stay again.
Great place, lovely staff, very clean and modern amenities, and the ground are well maintained, thank you.

We only visited the caravan park to see the Retro cars and caravans.  We did not stay. The park is in a great spot, with van sites and cabins.  It is deceptively large.  A very neat and tidy place that even has a pool.
Close to town, great atmosphere in a quiet caravan park.
Besides the couches in the lounge room bring uncomfortable.  The cottage over all was excellent.  The staff are absolutely terrific and very well kept caravan park very nest and tidy.

I recently stayed at the G'day Park a Proud supporter of Rural Aid:
Top of the Town Tourist Park whilst we were supporting local farmers affected by the Nov 23 bushfires.

The cabin was excellent, complete with a thoughtful check-in call.
The park's location allowed for sunrise views from Mt. Marlay lookout.

Thank you for your wonderful hospitality and support during our short stay. Highly recommended!

Nice park in a convenient location, it is a little undulating, making it difficult to level the caravan on some sites and we were there on a long weekend when it was absolutely packed and some of the sites are quite squishy. The amenities block is in great condition and kept well cleaned and maintained. And as usual for us it is pet friendly. So much to do around here, everything from the truffle farm, juicery, breweries, a whole lot of wineries and many other things.
Well kept caravan park.  Plenty of room to get a van onto a site.  Lovely clean amenities.
Nice caravan Park reasonably close to the main shopping area. You can walk there if you want to.
A very nice caravan park. Very clean amenities. Dog friendly.

Great van park. Nice roomie sites so you can put your car on the other side of the slab. Always a bonus. Amenities were very clean and plenty of them. Great camp kitchens and BBQ sites dotted around the park AND it was close to everything 🩷
Absolutely love this caravan park and Stanthorpe.

Well maintained clean park which is dog friendly.
Will be back 😀
Nice quiet caravan park. Lovely big clean showers, amenities always clean. The back of the park has bush camping so makes for lovely walks.

Good location but we stayed in the cottage paying a pretty hefty price for what we got. There was mould in the shower that had definitely not been cleaned before us. Nothing to wipe down a surface with. Not even a broom to sweep up. Barely had the bare essentials. Only a drop of dishwashing soap left.

Nice park , with a large site , staff friendly , it’s in a great location to explore the area . I am not sure the toilet was cleaned daily , while the pool was clean , there was no rail to assist older patrons to get in  , and the time in the pool was spoiled by teenagers jumping from the rock 🪨 and ignoring you when you mentioned it was dangerous , families with young children had to get out .
Quiet spot, make sure you book ahead
Great Caravan park and very friendly staff. Pet friendly as well. Will definitely stay again

A vile place, completely run down to the point of being derelict.

Customer service was very bad. We were allocated a campervan spot right next to the function room where early 20 somethings were having a christmas party, drinking and karaoke. Obviously it was very loud that kept our young kids awake until 10:15pm.

After contacting reception, we were told they can be as loud as they like until 10pm. As for us, tough luck! Fair enough you have christmas parties but do you really put a family with young children right next to it instead of moving them to a space well away.

Loved being woken up the next morning at 4:50am with the same party music thumping out until 5:15am.

Poor attitude, poor service and very dilapidated place, yuk!

Powered caravan sit was great. Receptionist was well mannered and very helpful. Park is kept neat and tidy and pool clean and refreshing. Location is prime when exploring the region and sights. Restaurant in walking distance if you don't want to cook.

What a peaceful place... walking around the Hida old town was so  relaxing after Takayama. We look for authentic experienced. If you go to Toyama hop a train to Hida then takayama. You will wish you stayed in Hida like we did. ||You can also hop on a local bus fron Takayama about 380 yen one way 40 minutes. (Nori Bus co.)  ||Takayama is geared towards tourism. However Due to lack of staff availability many shops restaurants have changing opening hours which was  frustrating.. mostly closed.

Spent 4 nights here.
The park has all the amenities which are clean and modern.
It is a quiet park close to town.
Would I stay here again, yes I would without a doubt.
Thanks guys
Bob and Archie the boxer
Great place very friendly
Surprisingly good facilities for the kids and the adults
Good location. Next to the show grounds. Well managed park. New washing machines recently installed.

The snug I booked was just perfect for the overnight stay I had.

Would recommend these guys for your stay.
Lovely powered site not far from town.  Amenities first rate - new & very clean 😊

Fantastic place. Plenty of different types of accommodation. Toilets amd shower extremely clean. Make sure you book well in advance as is often booked out. Close to town and Sunday markets next door 2nd and last Sunday od each month.

Good sized sites, huge park, great views.
Amenities were awesome, spotless, plenty of sinks, shower cubicles and toilets.
Large amount of sites with amazing views.
Just not many bins.

Stayed in a cabin. Clean but bare with most basic of amenities provided. Asked for a long life milk for the second day and was given it most reluctantly with the comment, we don't supply the milk for the second night stay.
Nice big caravan / motor home / big rig park. Clean big amenities. Plenty for the kids to do. Super friendly helpful staff.

Spent one night here in our caravan.
Great spot. Spotless amenities. Would recommend.
Stayed for 4 nights in an unpowered campsite. It had great amenities, clean toilets, and showers. We definitely enjoyed our stay here. No dramas. Good customer service.
Unpowered site at back of park. Stunning views. Felt like camping in the bush with all of the amenities of a well run and clean caravan park. Perfect.

Have always loved staying at this lovely caravan park. However, having to listen to uncontrolled yapping dogs, spoilt it for us this time. For some reason , some people with small dogs think continuous yapping is okay???? Didn't hear any if the larger dogs barking at all.
On site cabin was in very poor condition, extremely small inside and run down. Would not recommend.
Very friendly and helpful
Spacious caravan park. Super clean amenities block & very friendly helpful staff. Lovely camp kitchens. Definitely recommend.
